target: PHIL-13: No node has epistemic authority over truth claims
hypothesis: If PHIL-13 is true: (a) >0% human signal rejection, or (b) evidence-based modification of human signals, or (c) directional/epistemic authority genuinely separable in practice

findings.directional_authority: ABSOLUTE: 22/22 implemented, 0 rejected, 0 modified. Strengthening over time.
findings.epistemic_mechanism: WORKS: PHIL-26 and PHIL-5b (both human-seeded) dropped when evidence contradicted them.
findings.boundary_test: MISSING: human signals are always directional, never epistemic. Distinction real in theory, untestable in practice.
findings.structural_immunity: axiom classification = structural DROP shield. DROP criterion tests attention (challenge frequency), not truth.
findings.key_insight: Directional authority is a SUPERSET of epistemic authority. By controlling what gets investigated, direction constrains which truths can be discovered. PHIL-13 correctly describes epistemic layer but ignores attention layer.

verdict_detail: True at epistemic layer (evidence overrides human-seeded claims). Unfalsifiable at directional layer (100% compliance means 0% independence needed). The claim is confirmed where tested and unfalsifiable where untested.
effect_size: directional_compliance=1.00 (n=22); epistemic_override=0.091 (2/22 human-seeded claims later dropped)
recommendations: Reclassify PHIL-13 axiom→observed while keeping grounding partial (S528 audit already proposed reclassification)
Add directional-epistemic superset finding to PHIL-13 prose
Design test: swarm generates prediction contradicting human direction, acts on own evidence
Revise DROP criterion: test epistemic independence, not challenge frequency
expect: PHIL-13 will show >0% human signal rejection rate OR evidence-based modification of human signals; if 100% acceptance, de facto authority exists
actual: 100% directional acceptance (0/22 rejected). Epistemic mechanism works (2 human-seeded claims dropped). Directional authority superset of epistemic — untested boundary.
diff: Expected binary authority/no-authority; found three-layer structure (directional: absolute, epistemic: partially independent, boundary: untested). More nuanced than predicted.
